agent_annotation: Type VI secretion system–associated protein encoded in the imp gene cluster (adjacent to ImpA/ImpM/ImpL/IcmF-like components), likely contributing to T6SS apparatus assembly or function.
agent_evidence: Multiple ~44–47% identity homologs occur in conserved type VI secretion system (imp) neighborhoods: flanked by KEGG-annotated ImpA and membrane complex components ImpM and ImpL (ImpL annotated as IcmF-related) across Herbaspirillum and Ralstonia strains, consistent with a T6SS structural/accessory gene. RB-TnSeq fitness for homologs shows modestly increased fitness in several growth conditions (e.g., various carbon sources; nitrate stress), consistent with loss of a costly secretion system component being advantageous under laboratory conditions, supporting assignment to T6SS rather than core metabolism.
